name: pdfAvaluacioElize.pdf
content:
Term report (3rd term)

Data
Name:              Elise Walter-Tossens
Teacher:           Theresa Galler
Level:            Oliver 2024/2025
Absences:         0
Frequency of use (audio):   Pass

Participation/Motivation
Assessment (Participation / Motivation):  Good
Comment:            Elise shows a positive eagerness to engage in her learning, especially during activities like the dice game, where she demonstrated enthusiasm and presence. Despite facing some challenges with vocabulary, she enjoys participating in classroom tasks and activities. Her interests in games and hands-on materials, like flashcards, indicate her motivation to connect with the learning material. These interests can be harnessed to further enhance her learning experience and maintain her enthusiasm.

Learning process
Assessment (Learning process):  Good
Comment:            Elise is currently facing some challenges with vocabulary acquisition and constructing complete sentences, as she is still working on concepts related to clothing and prepositions. However, she demonstrates significant progress, particularly with prepositions like 'there is' and 'there are'. She performed well on worksheets, indicating a solid understanding of certain exercises. Continuous practice with vocabulary and sentence construction will be essential for her growth moving forward.

Behaviour
Assessment (Behaviour):  Satisfactory
Comment:            Elise is a friendly and kind student who readily shares materials and assists her classmates, contributing positively to the classroom environment. However, she often needs reminders to focus and tends to chat with peers during lessons, which can disrupt her learning. Despite these distractions, Elise responds well to guidance and refocusing prompts. Overall, her behaviour is manageable, and with continued support, she can further enhance her classroom conduct while maintaining her sociable nature.

Marks
Oral Test Mark:     7.70  Good. Elise can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

---

Final comment
Elise has shown a willingness to improve her behaviour. Throughout the year, she has demonstrated kindness and a willingness to help her peers, contributing positively to the classroom. Continued focus on vocabulary and sentence structure will aid her learning. As summer approaches, consistent listening to audio resources will assist her development and readiness for the upcoming year.

name: pdfAvaluacioMaya.pdf
content:
Term report (3rd term)

Data
Name:                    Maya Spineux
Teacher:                 Theresa Galler
Level:                   Oliver 2024/2025
Absences:                1
Frequency of use (audio):  Pass

Participation/Motivation
Assessment (Participation / Motivation):     Good
Comment:               Maya comes to class with a cheerful disposition, always smiling and eager to begin her lessons. Her enthusiasm is infectious, and she shows good motivation towards her work, particularly enjoying activities like Pass the Present and Who's Missing. Although she faced challenges with vocabulary and constructing complete sentences, her strong willingness to engage is commendable. Maya participates actively during games and enjoys singing, which reflects her positive approach towards learning.

Learning process
Assessment (Learning process):           Good
Comment:               Maya has made notable progress in her learning, participating well during exercises such as true or false and memory activities. She made good progress in warm-up activities, showing good grasp of concepts like weather-related tasks. However, she struggled with vocabulary and sentence construction at times. To enhance her learning further, continued practice in repeating vocabulary and engaging in fun exercises will be beneficial in reinforcing her knowledge.

Behaviour
Assessment (Behaviour):                     Good
Comment:               Maya exhibits friendly and cooperative behaviour in class, getting along well with both her classmates and teachers. Her cheerful nature contributes positively to the classroom environment. While she has shown occasional distractions requiring gentle reminders to refocus, she actively engages in group activities and willingly shares materials. Maya has been helpful in supporting her peers, demonstrating kindness and respect in her interactions, which enhances the overall classroom atmosphere.

Marks
Oral Test Mark:                7.20  Good. Maya can understand the question and answers either with a single word or with a structure which is only partially correct.

---

Final comment
Maya has had a productive year, demonstrating enthusiasm across a range of activities. While her vocabulary and sentence construction need improvement, her eagerness to participate is commendable. Encouraging her to listen to audio tracks over the summer will surely support her learning progress for next year.

name: pdfAvaluacioHugo.pdf
content:
Term report (3rd term)

Data
Name:                Hugo Theunissen
Teacher:             Theresa Galler
Level:               Oliver 2024/2025
Absences:            1
Frequency of use (audio):  Insufficient

Participation/Motivation
Assessment (Participation / Motivation):  Good
Comment:              Hugo demonstrates a good level of motivation during class activities, showing adequate participation and enthusiasm in distinguishing between True and False. He especially enjoyed the dice game and Passing the Present. Although he occasionally struggles to stay awake during story time, he does engage when he is alert. Hugo's willingness to participate, even when needing extra time to repeat answers, is commendable and he contributes positively to the classroom environment.

Learning process
Assessment (Learning process):  Satisfactory
Comment:              Hugo has shown satisfactory progress in his learning, particularly with true or false exercises and understanding worksheet activities. He performed adequately with vocabulary tasks, although he occasionally appears tired, affecting his engagement during stories. While he successfully repeats instructions when prompted, continued practice with full sentences will enhance his language skills further.

Behaviour
Assessment (Behaviour):  Good
Comment:              Hugo has a good rapport with peers and respects his teacher, which contributes to a pleasant classroom environment. He displays both kindness and respect for learning materials, vital for maintaining a positive environment. Furthermore, he's capable of taking turns, underscoring his understanding of cooperative conduct in group activities.

Marks
Oral Test Mark:      6.90  Acceptable. Hugo can understand the question but, in most cases, still does not respond with a complete structure.

---

Final comment
Hugo has had a good year, demonstrating good participation and developing his skills in various activities. He should continue to listen to audio tracks over the summer to assist in further developing his English skills. This will help him be more engaged and ready for the challenges of the coming year. Overall, Hugo's positive interactions and effort are commendable.
